response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist engineer with deep TypeScript/Node.js and DLang experience, but falls well short of the mandatory requirements: professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments (2014-2015) versus the required 5-10 years, there is no evidence of Windows-platform C++ work, no SQL Server or Oracle experience (only MySQL/MongoDB), and no fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain exposure — RIDE Capital was retail investment fintech, not institutional capital markets.
strengths: Solid systems-programming background (DLang low-latency services, C/C++ at National Instruments)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing tooling author
Fluent English and proven communication skills (workshops, talks, courses)
concerns: C++ experience is brief (~7 months professionally) and dated, far below the 5-10 year requirement
No fixed income, derivatives, or investment banking / capital markets domain experience
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; recent stack is entirely web/Node.js, not Windows C++

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain
